goal: Remove custom player swim physics so the player can use Roblox-native water swimming inside the filled cell volume.
changes: Changed the main cell movement mode from CytoplasmSwimming to NativeWaterSwimming.
Stopped calling the custom MainCellSwimmingController installer from BuildMainCellExperience.build().
Added installNativeWaterSwimmingMode() to delete custom player LocalScripts from StarterPlayerScripts and PlayerScripts.

Added runtime cleanup for custom player physics objects: MainCellCytoplasmBuoyancy, MainCellSwimVelocity, MainCellSwimOrientation, MainCellSwimAttachment, CytoplasmBuoyancy, CytoplasmVelocity, and CytoplasmOrientation.
Removed custom touch dive GUI artifacts from active players.
Restored Workspace.Gravity to Roblox default 196.2 for native water and object float tests.
Left non-player scene objects and nucleus assemblies untouched.
validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio through the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() in Studio and confirmed NativeWaterSwimming mode.
Queried StarterPlayerScripts and runtime players; no custom player swim scripts or physics artifacts remained.
important_observation: Workspace.Nucleus_Model is currently fully anchored and non-colliding. It will not demonstrate water floatability until it is converted to an unanchored welded assembly or given a separate physical proxy.
next_recommended_phase: If the goal is to test nucleus float/sink behavior, create a single unanchored nucleus physics proxy or welded assembly while preserving the visual nucleus meshes.
